page: Google Cloud Platform c3d-standard-16 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance featuring 16 vCPUs and 64 GB of RAM. Powered by an x86_64 AMD EPYC 9B14 processor, the instance allocates 8 physical cores with 2 threads per core on a dedicated basis. The CPU cache configuration includes 32 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. It supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 (including VNNI and BF16) and cryptographic acceleration via AES, VAES, and SHA_NI. This instance does not include local NVMe storage or bundled GPUs.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core CPU capabilities, with stress-ng single-core performance and Geekbench text processing scoring in the top 25%. Multi-core operations, including stress-ng multi-core, Passmark CPU, and software compilation with Clang, fall within the average range. Memory performance is mixed; while cached small-block read bandwidth is average, uncached large-block read and write bandwidths are weak to poor, representing a potential bottleneck for memory-intensive workloads. Large-model LLM inference is also limited, though text generation with small models performs in the top 25%.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c3d-standard-16 offers a balanced resource profile suitable for standard enterprise workloads without the premium of specialized hardware. Given its balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and solid single-core performance, it is well-suited for general-purpose applications, web serving, database operations, text processing, and light machine learning inference using smaller models.
description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-standard-16 is a general-purpose virtual machine configured with 16 vCPUs and 64 GB of RAM. It is powered by an AMD EPYC 9B14 x86_64 processor with 8 physical cores and dedicated CPU allocation. The hardware features 32 MB of L3 cache and supports AVX-512 and cryptographic instructions. Benchmark data shows strong single-core CPU performance and text processing, while multi-core execution, database operations, and web serving throughput remain average. However, uncached large-block memory read and write bandwidths are weak to poor, indicating a tradeoff for memory-bound applications. Lacking local storage and GPUs, this instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for balanced workloads. It is best suited for general-purpose applications, web serving, text processing, and small-model machine learning inference.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-standard-16 is a general-purpose instance with 16 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC 9B14 CPU, delivering strong single-core performance for balanced enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-standard-16 features 16 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC CPU, optimized for balanced general-purpose workloads.
tagline: A balanced x86_64 virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processing and strong single-core performance for general-purpose enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 16 vCPUs and 64 GB RAM powered by an AMD EPYC 9B14 processor
Strong single-core CPU performance and text processing capabilities
Supports AVX-512 instruction sets and cryptographic acceleration
Average multi-core, database, and web serving benchmark results
Weak uncached large-block memory bandwidth performance
Best suited for general-purpose applications and web serving
